input.title: Legal Practice Management System
input.description: Legal Practice Management System is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

scope: Case Timeline and Activity Log workflows
constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Python Django, PostgreSQL for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Plan Client Intake and Registration Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for client intake and registration and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Implement complete client intake and registration functionality; All client intake and registration operations must be validated and authorized; System shall maintain audit log for client intake and registration actions.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for client intake and registration is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for client intake and registration are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for client intake and registration can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Client Intake and Registration Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for client intake and registration on React, Python Django, PostgreSQL. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Implement complete client intake and registration functionality; All client intake and registration operations must be validated and authorized; System shall maintain audit log for client intake and registration actions.
acceptance_criteria: Core client intake and registration business rules are implemented for Legal Practice Management System
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for client intake and registration
API/service behavior for client intake and registration is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Client Intake and Registration Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for client intake and registration with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The client intake and registration user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for client intake and registration behave correctly
The client intake and registration UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Harden Client Intake and Registration Security and Access Rules
FR-01.description: Apply security controls to client intake and registration. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for client intake and registration are enforced consistently across API and UI layers
Sensitive operations and data paths in client intake and registration are audited and protected against misuse
Security validation for client intake and registration passes review and automated checks

FR-01.title: Write Client Intake and Registration Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for client intake and registration. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for client intake and registration are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for client intake and registration are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical client intake and registration scenarios
